title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 18, 2026: Markets Rebound Amid Geopolitical Easing and Tech Strength

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally as Iran Peace Deal and Tech Gains Boost Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. equity markets opened sharply higher on Thursday, reversing some of the previous day's losses. Investor confidence was buoyed by the formal signing of an interim peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran, easing geopolitical tensions and driving oil prices lower. Technology stocks led the advance, reflecting a renewed appetite for artificial intelligence and semiconductor companies.

items.headline: Intel Soars on Apple Chip Deal News
items.summary: Intel shares surged after President Donald Trump announced the semiconductor giant agreed to partner with Apple to design chips in the U.S.

items.headline: Smith & Wesson Brands Jumps on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Smith & Wesson Brands surged after reporting better-than-expected fourth-quarter results.

items.headline: FreeCast Rockets on Starlink Deal
items.summary: Streaming stock FreeCast saw its shares rocket following a new Starlink broadband deal.

items.headline: Accenture Tumbles on Weak Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Accenture shares tumbled after the IT services company provided a fourth-quarter revenue forecast that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

items.headline: NovoCure Limited Falls in Premarket Trading
items.summary: NovoCure Limited experienced a significant drop in premarket trading.

items.headline: SpaceX Continues Decline Post-IPO
items.summary: SpaceX continued its decline, falling for the second straight day since its public debut last week.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Maintains Rates, Signals Potential Hikes Ahead
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% after its June 16-17 meeting. However, under new Chair Kevin Warsh, policymakers adopted a more hawkish tone, projecting the possibility of further rate hikes later in 2026 due to persistent inflation concerns.

items.headline: Oil Prices Fall as U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement Signed
items.summary: Brent crude prices fell below $80 a barrel, reflecting a significant decline from recent highs, following the signing of an interim peace deal between the U.S. and Iran. This agreement is expected to ease geopolitical risks and improve global energy supply.

items.headline: Technology Sector Drives Market Rebound; Chip Stocks Surge
items.summary: The technology-focused Nasdaq Composite outperformed, with the sector leading overall market gains. A renewed appetite for artificial intelligence, semiconductor, and software companies, coupled with news of an Intel-Apple chip manufacturing partnership, fueled the rally.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Rise, Philly Fed Index Rebounds
items.summary: Initial Jobless Claims came in slightly above estimates at 226,000, marking the third consecutive week of elevated claims. Conversely, the Philly Fed Manufacturing Index bounced back into positive territory for June at +10.3, following a negative reading in May.
